assumptions: The question concerns consolidated financial statements.
The intragroup dividends give rise to a recognised intragroup monetary receivable/payable with foreign currency exposure.
The issue is whether that foreign currency exposure can be addressed through IFRS 9 hedge accounting in consolidation.

approaches.label: Cash flow hedge accounting
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 identifies cash flow hedge accounting as a top-level hedge accounting model and specifically addresses when intragroup foreign currency exposures may qualify in consolidated financial statements.

approaches.label: Fair value hedge accounting
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 identifies fair value hedge accounting as a distinct top-level hedge accounting model for recognised assets or liabilities and firm commitments.

approaches.label: Hedge of a net investment in a foreign operation
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: The provided context includes IFRS 9 and IFRIC 16 guidance on net investment hedges as a separate hedge accounting model for foreign currency risk in consolidated financial statements.

approaches.label: No hedge accounting
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: The context distinguishes exposures that qualify for hedge accounting from those that do not, making ordinary accounting without hedge accounting a separate relevant top-level treatment.
